Residential Tenancy Agreement

This Tenancy Agreement is made on [Agreement Date] between:

Landlord [Landlord Full Name or Company Name]
Landlord Address [Landlord Address]
Tenant [Tenant Full Name]
Property [Rental Property Address]
Tenancy Start Date [Start Date]
Fixed Term [e.g. 12 months]
Rent [Amount] per calendar month, payable on [Rent Due Date]
Deposit [Deposit Amount]

1. Grant of Tenancy

The Landlord lets the Property to the Tenant for the term described above, subject to the terms of this Agreement.

2. Rent and Payments

  1. The Tenant shall pay the rent in full and on time.
  2. The Tenant shall pay any other sums due under this Agreement when they fall due.
  3. The Landlord shall provide payment instructions and may update them from time to time.

3. Deposit

The deposit shall be held and administered in accordance with applicable law. Deductions may be made for unpaid rent, damage beyond fair wear and tear, cleaning, or other lawful losses.

4. Tenant Obligations

  1. Keep the Property reasonably clean and ventilated.
  2. Promptly report repairs, leaks, safety issues, and damage.
  3. Not cause nuisance, annoyance, or unlawful activity at the Property.
  4. Not alter, damage, or remove fixtures without written permission.
  5. Allow reasonable access for inspections, repairs, and legal obligations with appropriate notice, except in emergencies.

5. Landlord Obligations

  1. Keep in repair the structure and exterior of the Property as required by law.
  2. Maintain installations for water, gas, electricity, sanitation, space heating, and hot water as required by law.
  3. Give the Tenant quiet enjoyment of the Property, subject to this Agreement and legal rights of entry.

6. Utilities and Council Tax

Responsibility for utilities, council tax, internet, television licence, and other services shall be as follows: [Insert allocation of responsibility].

7. Use of the Property

  1. The Property shall be used only as a private residence unless otherwise agreed in writing.
  2. The Tenant shall not assign, sublet, or part with possession without written consent.
  3. The Tenant shall comply with building rules and estate regulations where applicable.

8. Ending the Tenancy

This Agreement may be ended in accordance with the fixed term, any break clause, notice requirements, and applicable law.

9. Inventory and Condition

The parties may sign or acknowledge a separate inventory, check-in report, or schedule of condition forming part of the tenancy records.

10. Data and Notices

The parties may exchange tenancy-related notices, payment reminders, repair communication, and document requests through Tenaqo, email, or any other agreed communication method.

11. Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, unless the parties expressly agree otherwise in writing.

Signed by the Landlord Name: [Landlord Signatory Name]
Title: [Title, if company]
Date: [Date] 		Signed by the Tenant Name: [Tenant Name]
Date: [Date]
Usage note: Replace the bracketed placeholders before upload, or leave signature names and dates for provider fields. In DocuSeal or DocuSign, add signature and date fields in the two signature blocks.

This is a working starting point, not legal advice. Review it for deposit handling, notices, prescribed information, break clauses, guarantors, and any jurisdiction-specific requirements before production use.
